ace: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Acehnese.
act: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Achterhooks.
activate: verb. To encourage development or induce increased activity; to stimulate.
active: adjective. Having the power or quality of acting; causing change; communicating action or motion; acting;, opposed to passive, that
ait: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Arikem.
ate: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Atemble.
att: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pamplona Atta.
attic: noun. The space, often unfinished and with sloped walls, directly below the roof in the uppermost part of a house or other building,
ava: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Avar.
ave: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Avestan.
cat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Catalan.
cate: noun. A delicacy or item of food.
cattie: noun. Archaic form of catty (“Chinese unit of weight”).
cave: noun. A large, naturally-occurring cavity formed underground or in the face of a cliff or a hillside.
cavitate: verb. To form vapour bubbles in a flowing liquid in a region where the pressure of the liquid falls below its vapour pressure.
cite: verb. To quote; to repeat, as a passage from a book, or the words of another.
civet: noun. Any of the small carnivorous catlike mammals encompassing certain species from the families Viverridae, Eupleridae, and Nandiniidae,
eat: verb. To ingest; to be ingested.
eta: noun. The seventh letter of the Modern Greek alphabet, the eighth in Old Greek.
etic: adjective. Of or pertaining to analysis of a culture from a perspective situated outside all cultures.
evict: verb. To expel (one or more people) from their property; to force (one or more people) to move out.
ice: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Icelandic.
tacet: verb. An instruction indicating silence on the part of the performers of a piece.
tacit: adjective. Implied, but not made explicit, especially through silence.
tact: noun. Sensitive mental touch; special skill or faculty; keen perception or discernment; ready power of appreciating and doing what is
tae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tariana.
tat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tatar.
tate: noun. Of video games, a vertical mode.
tav: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tatuyo.
tea: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Temiar.
teat: noun. The projection of a mammary gland from which, on female therian mammals, milk is secreted.

tic: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tira.
tit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tinigua.
vac: noun. Clipping of vacation (“extended period of time away from work or school”).

via: noun. A main road or highway, especially in ancient Rome. (Mainly used in set phrases, below.)
vice: noun. Bad or immoral behaviour. (Especially often, a habit that harms oneself or others)
vie: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Vietnamese.

vitta: noun. A fillet, or garland for the head.
